channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders are negotiating a deal to hire Pete Carroll as their head coach, AP source says
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders are negotiating a deal to hire Pete Carroll to be their new head coach, a person with knowledge of the decision told The Associated Press on Friday. The person spoke on condition of anonymity because the contract hasn’t been finalized.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vegas, baby! Sin City to host the 2027 College Football Playoff national championship game
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Las Vegas will host the 2027 College Football Playoff national championship game. The CFP made the announcement Friday, saying the title game will be played Monday, Jan. 25, 2027, at Allegiant Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Patriots safety Jabrill Peppers testifies that he didn't choke or shove his accuser
item.description: New England Patriots safety Jabrill Peppers has finished testifying in his assault and battery trial after denying he choked or shoved his accuser.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: F1 champion Max Verstappen the brightest star missing from stacked field at Rolex 24 of Daytona
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The notorious tune “Du du du duh Max Verstappen ...” is likely loathed by every racing driver other than the reigning four-time Formula 1 champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The ties between soccer and the NBA are real, and they're still growing
item.description: PARIS (AP) — San Antonio star Victor Wembanyama stood outside in the rain on a cold Paris night. He wore a parka, a hooded sweatshirt, jeans and sneakers. And then he spent a few seconds showing off his skills. A basketball was not involved.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Prep roundup: Evergreen Valley girls turn back Willow Glen; Salesian boys dominate another league foe
item.description: Piedmont, Bishop O'Dowd, Moreau Catholic, Westmont girls, St. Mary's-Berkeley, O'Dowd boys among Thursday's winners.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lefkow, Correspondent, The Mercury News

item.title: Wrexham's celebrity owners will have to wait to see their team playing in Europe
item.description: Wrexham competes in English soccer's league system but the Welsh soccer club owned by Hollywood celebrities Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney was hoping to be allowed to participate in the Welsh League Cup.
item.dc:creator: Steve Douglas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Novak Djokovic vows to strive for more Grand Slam titles at age 37 after his latest injury
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — It could be tempting to write off Novak Djokovic at this point, to think he might be closer than ever to being done competing for Grand Slam titles and closer than ever to retirement, where his greatest on-court rivals — Roger Federer, Rafael Nadal and Andy Murray — alrea
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Djokovic quits mid-match and walks off to boos, putting Zverev in Australian Open final vs. Sinner
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Spectators booed an injured Novak Djokovic as he left the court in Rod Laver Arena after quitting one set into his Australian Open semifinal against Alexander Zverev on Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Purdy: Retracing the 49ers’ redemption path to Super Bowl XIX, 40 years later
item.description: "Every Sunday when quarterback Joe Montana took his first snap, it was like watching someone pull a trigger on a weapon that couldn’t be stopped. Every Sunday when the 49ers’ defense lined up with skill and intent, opponents went nowhere.
item.dc:creator: Mark Purdy, The Mercury News

item.title: Jannik Sinner beats Ben Shelton to return to the Australian Open final
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Defending champion Jannik Sinner overcame some third-set cramping and beat Ben Shelton 7-6 (2), 6-2, 6-2 on Friday to return to the Australian Open final as he seeks a third Grand Slam title. The No.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Recording captured ex-interpreter impersonating Ohtani to transfer $200,000, prosecutors say
item.description: Federal prosecutors say a nearly four-minute audio recording allegedly captured Shohei Ohtani's former interpreter Ippei Mizuhara impersonating the baseball star on a call with a bank as he attempted to transfer $200,000.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia high school basketball player assaults pair of athletes from opposing team, video shows
item.description: Social media video shows a few of the chaotic moments that occurred in the second half of a basketball game between two high school teams in Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bills might be better, but Chiefs are favored for a reason
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The Chiefs are slight favorites to beat the Bills on Sunday and get a shot at winning an unprecedented third consecutive Super Bowl. But that doesn't mean Kansas City is the better team. “I agree with that,” CBS SportsLine handicapper Bruce Marshall said.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayor Whitmire to send food to Kansas City following Chiefs’ win—under the condition that the game’s refs get a share
item.description: Houston Mayor John Whitmire is a man of his word. He’s sending some of Houston’s best food to Kansas City after the Chiefs’ big win and making sure that the people who made it all possible get a plate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Horton, KPRC 2

item.title: Woman indicted in car crash that killed Vikings rookie Khyree Jackson and 2 others
item.description: UPPER MARLBORO, Md. (AP) — A woman was indicted Thursday in the July 6 car crash that killed Minnesota Vikings rookie cornerback Khyree Jackson and two of his former high school teammates.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL legend Champ Bailey dishes on breaking stigma with cannabis, using while playing in league
item.description: Pro Football Hall of Famer Champ Bailey talked to Fox News Digital about his cannabis advocacy and why he uses the drug as well as using it during his NFL career.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ESPN analyst reveals how ex-colleague's Obama comments became point of contention
item.description: Ryan Clark hopped on Michele Tafoya's podcast to discuss his "only" issue with Sage Steele and why, at one point, he did not want to work with her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oakland Roots soccer team eyes short-term home at Coliseum
item.description: (BCN) -- The Oakland Roots professional soccer team wants to continue playing at the Coliseum for the "short-term" following the 2025 season, according to team officials.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Evans' goal late in the third period completes Canadiens' rally past Lightning in 3-2 victory
item.description: Jake Evans scored the winning goal with 2:15 remaining to rally the Montreal Canadiens past the Tampa Bay Lightning 3-2 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles 11-year-old pulls one-of-a-kind baseball card
item.description: An 11-year-old baseball card collector from Los Angeles just pulled what is likely the most important card they will ever pull. Topps, the card manufacturer, confirmed Tuesday morning that the Paul Skenes 1/1 Debut Patch card was in fact unpacked. The card features the patch that the 2023 No.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio State wins 1st national title since 2014, outlasting Notre Dame 34-23 in CFP championship game
item.description: Instead of crying over another collapse, Ohio State can celebrate another national title after holding off a Notre Dame comeback bid Monday night to walk away with a nailbiter of a 34-23 victory over the Fighting Irish.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Barkley runs for 78- and 62-yard TDs in the snow and Eagles top Rams 28-22 to head to NFC title game
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley blew through a hole and ran untouched in the wintry mix, the Eagles' 2,000-yard rusher smacking his helmet with his hand on his final snowy steps of a 78-yard touchdown run.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers continue offseason dominance, sign All-Star reliever
item.description: As the Los Angeles Dodgers stormed toward the World Series last October, one weakness was consistently brought up for the Boys in Blue: relief pitching.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Runnin' Utes hold off BYU in overtime, 73-72
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Ezra Ausar scored a career-high 26 points on 11-of-15 shooting and Hunter Erickson scored Utah's last six points on Saturday night to help the Runnin' Utes beat BYU 73-72 in overtime.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
